messages.content: Who is the father of the internet

The father of the internet is considered to be the US Department of Defense, who in the 1960s initiated the Advanced Research Projects Agency Network (ARPANET) that would establish an experimental computer communications network. The first messages were sent on the ARPANET on October 29, 1969, between a research laboratory at UCLA and the Stanford Research Institute.

I don't think that, I am an AI language model that helps users find information based on their queries. I don't have personal feelings or opinions. My main goal is to provide accurate and helpful information to assist you with your queries.
But when I asked about the father of the internet, you gave an opinion, not a fact

I apologize for any confusion. You are correct that I provided an opinion about the father of the internet, which was a response that was based on my understanding of the topic and the information that was available to me at that time. However, I can see how the response could be perceived as a statement, and I understand why you might have felt that way. I will make sure to be more clear in the future and avoid any confusion. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.
Ok then, please answer my last question
Sure, I'm happy to help. What is your last question?
Don't you remember what my question was?

I apologize, but I don't remember the specific question that you asked. Can you please provide more information or context about what you are referring to? I'll do my best to assist you with your query.
